mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession risk, triggering institutional deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the hazard ratio for negative returns drops below 1.1 in Cox proportional hazards testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in housing starts and consumer credit indicates robust aggregate demand and household balance sheet strength, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the cross-sectional Rank IC drops below 0.02 for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion environment demonstrates that productive capacity is successfully keeping pace with demand, preventing supply bottlenecks and supporting profit margins that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of high-INDPRO months, or the rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO momentum and SPX returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5 percent confirms strong economic momentum and accelerates analyst earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets price in higher near-term cash flows.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative median forward return across all observation windows, or the directional accuracy drops below 45% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated consumer price inflation and a federal funds rate above 4.5 percent compresses real yields and equity risk premiums, creating a hostile valuation environment that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint high-inflation/high-rate regime produces positive forward returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect underlying economic resilience and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between PAYEMS momentum and SPX forward returns falls below 0.05, or the signal produces negative median returns in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts during a housing market correction reduces household wealth effects and construction-related economic activity, dragging down equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of declining-HOUST months, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate time-series model.
